root_cause.text: The WebSocket frame parser in Apache Tomcat failed to validate that the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length was zero, allowing illegal negative payload lengths to be processed.
root_cause.evidence: The patch in `WsFrameBase.java` adds a check `if (payloadLength < 0)` when decoding an 8-byte payload length (when initial length is 127), throwing a protocol error if the condition is met, and the added comment explicitly references RFC 6455 section 5.2 which requires this bit to be zero.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The WebSocket frame parser in Apache Tomcat failed to validate that the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload length was zero, allowing illegal negative payload lengths to be processed.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch in `WsFrameBase.java` adds a check `if (payloadLength < 0)` when decoding an 8-byte payload length (when initial length is 127), throwing a protocol error if the condition is met, and the added comment explicitly references RFC 6455 section 5.2 which requires this bit to be zero.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add localized error message for invalid 64-bit WebSocket frame payloads.
intent_record.what: Added a new error message property for invalid WebSocket payload lengths.
intent_record.how: Enables the application to log or return a specific error when the most significant bit of a 64-bit payload is illegally set.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add boundary check for negative payload length in WebSocket frames.
intent_record.what: Added a check to throw a WsIOException if the parsed 8-byte payloadLength is negative.
intent_record.how: Prevents further processing of WebSocket frames with invalid payload lengths by throwing an exception.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update changelog to document added validation for WebSocket message payload lengths.
intent_record.what: Added a new subsection to the changelog XML file to document a fix for bug 64563.
intent_record.how: Updates project documentation to inform users about added validation for WebSocket message payload lengths without altering executable code.

grouping_result.intent: Update changelog to document added validation for WebSocket message payload lengths.
Add boundary check for negative payload length in WebSocket frames.

consistency_review.core_intent: Add validation and documentation for WebSocket message payload lengths.

relevance_review.reason: The group adds the specific localized error message for invalid 64-bit WebSocket frame payloads and updates the changelog to document this fix, directly addressing the vulnerability's root cause.
The group directly addresses the root cause by adding a boundary check for negative payload lengths in WebSocket frames, which was the missing validation causing the vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line reads the 64-bit payload length, which subsequently lacks validation for negative values (most significant bit set to 1).
Continuation of the line reading the 64-bit payload length before the missing validation.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement reads the 64-bit payload length, which lacks validation for negative values (most significant bit set to 1).
This statement is the continuation of the payloadLength assignment, which is immediately followed by the missing validation in the fixed version.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
Websocket read implementation now uses SocketWrapperBase.read(boolean, ByteBuffer)

git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/r
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit fc6f2ead8463d06ae73954508927e4f2224c8e77
Author: Violeta Georgieva Georgieva <violetagg@apache.org>
Date:   Mon Sep 26 11:42:26 2016 +0000

    Websocket read implementation now uses SocketWrapperBase.read(boolean, ByteBuffer)
    
    git-svn-id: https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/tomcat/trunk@1762324 13f79535-47bb-0310-9956-ffa450edef68

diff --git a/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java b/java/org/apache/tomcat/websocket/WsFrameBase.java
index 241a7f962d..84e8132e7d 100644
--
bic_trace_step.reason: The function calculates the 64-bit payload length using 'payloadLength = byteArrayToLong(..., 8);' when the length identifier is 127, but fails to check if the most significant bit is zero (meaning 'payloadLength' could be negative). This allows illegal negative payload lengths.
